===''Pokémon the Series''===
[[File:Byron anime.png|thumb|left|250px|Byron in {{aniseries|DP}}]]
====''Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl''====
Prior to ''[[DP015|Shapes of Things to Come!]]'', Byron left his position as the {{ci|Oreburgh}} [[Gym Leader]] to his son [[Roark]] after the [[Pokémon League]] informed him there was a vacancy at the [[Canalave Gym]]. The transfer also allowed Byron to continue his hobby of digging for [[fossil]]s on nearby [[Iron Island]], hence the [[Gym]]'s closure from ''[[DP104|Sleepless In Pre-Battle!]]'' to ''[[DP107|Ancient Family Matters!]]''.

In ''Ancient Family Matters!'', Roark traveled to [[Canalave City]] to confront Byron over his absence from the family. The two argued, which escalated into a {{pkmn|battle}}. [[Byron's Bastiodon]] and [[Roark's Rampardos]] were evenly matched, but the battle was interrupted after {{TRT}} set off alarms when they stole Byron's fossil collection. They joined forces to defeat Team Rocket and {{AP|Pikachu}} sent Team Rocket blasting off. Later, Byron revealed that he had always treasured a fossil of a {{p|Sunkern}}'s leaf that Roark had given to him as a child ten years earlier. As a result, Roark accepted that his father did care about him all along.

In ''[[DP108|Dealing With Defensive Types!]]'', {{Ash}} had his [[Gym]] battle against Byron. {{AP|Chimchar}} defeated Byron's {{p|Bronzor}} but was left incredibly tired from the round. Ash called out his {{AP|Buizel}}, but it was surprised by {{p|Steelix}}'s {{m|Screech}} attack and quickly lost the match. Chimchar won the round after it weakened the battleground with {{m|Dig}}, and Steelix was left vulnerable to Chimchar's supereffective {{t|Fire}} attacks. Byron sent out his Bastiodon, and it knocked Chimchar out. In the final match, [[Ash's Gliscor]] defeated Bastiodon despite its showcased speed and resilience, earning Ash the {{Badge|Mine}}.

====[[Pokémon movie]]s====
Byron briefly appeared during the ending credits of ''[[M13|Zoroark: Master of Illusions]]''.

====Character====
Byron is prone to bragging about the ironclad {{stat|defense}}s of his Pokémon. He is extremely fond of and enthusiastic towards many things, often yelling how much he loves them at the top of his lungs. Byron's mostly defensive battling style makes it hard for opponents to break through and deal damage to his Pokémon, even if they have a [[type]] advantage.
